maml:description.maml:para: Get an Akamai bot category action.
Lists policy actions assigned to Akamai bot categories, or retrieves a single action for a category specified by `-CategoryID`.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID`. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your bot category ID.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID`. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Specifies the action that the rule should trigger. Either `alert`, `deny`, `deny_custom_{custom_deny_id}`, or `none`. Use `deny_custom_{custom_deny_id}` to apply a custom action instead of Akamai's default. If the action is `none`, it isn't associated with the policy.
Your bot category ID.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
